content: {"perspective": "Economic", "question": "Round 1 identified that the only published IF-specific economic evaluation is the trial-based and lifetime model-based cost-utility analysis of a monthly 5-day fasting-mimicking diet (FMD) programme in type 2 diabetes. What are the specific ICER per QALY, net monetary benefit, cost components, and probabilistic sensitivity analysis results of that study — and how do its assumptions about treatment effect durability (one-year trial extrapolated to lifetime) compare with methodological standards used in NICE and ICER base-case models for lifestyle interventions?", "search_queries": ["fasting mimicking diet FMD type 2 diabetes cost-effectiveness ICER QALY probabilistic sensitivity analysis", "FMD Prolon diabetes cost utility trial-based lifetime model Markov Netherlands", "fasting mimicking diet economic evaluation willingness to pay threshold QALY incremental cost", "fasting mimicking diet treatment effect durability extrapolation lifetime model assumption", "NICE lifestyle intervention cost-effectiveness treatment effect waning assumption base-case"]}
